title: Business Analyst
description:
Company Description

Experian is a global data and technology company, powering opportunities for people and businesses around the world. We help to redefine lending practices, uncover and prevent fraud, simplify healthcare, create marketing solutions, and gain deeper insights into the automotive market, all using our unique combination of data, analytics and software. We also assist millions of people to realize their financial goals and help them save time and money.

We operate across a range of markets, from financial services to healthcare, automotive, agribusiness, insurance, and many more industry segments.

We invest in people and new advanced technologies to unlock the power of data. As a FTSE 100 Index company listed on the London Stock Exchange (EXPN), we have a team of 22,500 people across 32 countries. Our corporate headquarters are in Dublin, Ireland. Learn more at experianplc.com.

Job Description

Job description

Job Summary


Develop required documentation such as workshop agenda, presentations, gap analysis reports, process flow diagrams, roles/responsibilities.
Identifying areas of process improvement (efficiency and effectiveness) and recommending solutions that detail pros, cons, and risks.
Promoting continuous process improvement practices through process metrics/KPIs, dashboards and role accountabilities.
Managing and communicating process and business requirements to the implementation team as a bridge to ensure that the proposed solutions meet the customer's expectations.
Participate in testing and ensure all the required documentation is in place to support the client and Managed Service team where needed.
Co-ordination and support of client UAT, verifying defects and enhancements and working with the development team to rectify.
Support client process governance activities, enabling the establishment of boards and management frameworks or adapting existing frameworks as appropriate.
Demonstrations of the product for customers
Proven experience in defining and deploying 'to be' best practice processes and in identifying solutions from a people, process, and technology perspective.
Excellent communication skills (both written and verbal) with strong presentation skills
Solid experience in requirements gathering, including experience in creating process documentation.
Demonstrate ability to influence and consult (providing options with pros, cons and risks) while providing thought leadership to stakeholders in solving governance, process or technical problems.
Strong interpersonal skills, customer centric attitude, ability to deal with cultural diversity. Proven team player and team builder.
Ability to effectively communicate with all levels of the organization
Ability to demonstrate analytical and problem-solving skills.
Ability to mentor novice staff and drive quality, value, and best practices


Qualifications

Qualifications


B. Tech in Computer Science
3 to 5 minimum ServiceNow process consultancy experience with recent focus on the ITOM module and related experience in ServiceNow Enterp Service Management
Hands-on ServiceNow experience meeting client requirements.
Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Management, Information Systems or related discipline preferred.
Drive ServiceNow (ITOM module) process definition, re-engineering, improvement, and gap analysis of current/to-be processes during client workshops with key customer sponsors and stakeholders.


Additional Information

Our uniqueness is that we celebrate yours. Experian's culture and people are important differentiators. We take our people agenda very seriously and focus on what matters; DEI, work/life balance, development, authenticity, collaboration, wellness, reward & recognition, volunteering... the list goes on. Experian's people first approach is award-winning; World's Best Workplaces™ 2024 (Fortune Top 25), Great Place To Work™ in 24 countries, and Glassdoor Best Places to Work 2024 to name a few. Check out Experian Life on social or our Careers Site to understand why.

Experian is proud to be an Equal Opportunity and Affirmative Action employer. Innovation is an important part of Experian's DNA and practices, and our diverse workforce drives our success. Everyone can succeed at Experian and bring their whole self to work, irrespective of their gender, ethnicity, religion, colour, sexuality, physical ability or age. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know at the earliest opportunity.

Experian Careers - Creating a better tomorrow together

Find out what its like to work for Experian by clicking here

title: Analyst- Strategy & Insights-Market Research & Analysis- Hyderabad
description:
Summary

Position Summary

Job Title: Analyst

The Team: GPS | Strategy & Insights (S&I)

The team

The GPS Strategy & Insights (S&I) team in Government & Public Services Enterprise Solutions fulfills essential functions in support of the practice. S&I is responsible for designing & executing growth strategies and market sensing activities for Deloitte’s Federal, State & Local, and Higher Education work. This includes bespoke strategy services for internal business customers, growth forecasting, evaluation of market positioning and sizing, competitive intelligence, disruptive trends evaluations, and investment business case development.

Job Summary

The Analyst is a dedicated position, supporting Innovation & Technology/assets portfolio within S&I full time. The Analyst leverages market expertise to lead and support strategy and research initiatives across the team.

Work you’ll do


Lead or support strategic engagements, market research and market sensing analysis, and priority projects and deliver high quality and insightful deliverables to senior leaders across the practice.
Conduct comprehensive market research to support assets within the GPS Innovation & Technology portfolio
Utilize analytical techniques and research tools to synthesize data from various sources, identify patterns and trends, and generate actionable insights that support Deloitte’s business decisions and strategies.
Translate numbers, market/industry trends, and data to consumable insights - both explanatory and exploratory.
Develop processes, models, and tools to inform GPS leaders and support overall growth.
Conduct financial analysis to assess the performance, profitability, and growth of the business, using metrics and KPIs such as revenue, expenses, margins, and ROI.
Examine data based on GPS finance KFIs (Key Financial Indicators) that align with the business objectives and goals, such as, revenue, forecast, budgeting, and acquisition cost.
Develop clear and compelling reports and dashboards that visualize data and insights in a user-friendly and intuitive way, using best practices and standards for data presentation and visualization.
Provide “so what” analysis that goes beyond describing what the data shows and explains why it matters, what are the implications, and what are the recommendations for action to the senior leadership.
Cultivate connections across the practice and continually bring new insights and perspectives into analysis efforts.
Mentor team members and wider practice as applicable to increase market knowledge and use of tools and resources.


Qualifications


Bachelor’s or master’s degree in business, Economics, Finance, or another similar technical discipline.
3-7 years of experience in market research and analysis, competitive intelligence, or similar strategic functions.
Knowledge of Deloitte’s GPS practice, sectors, offerings, and market dynamics.
Understanding of products/P&L/assets is preferred
Knowledge of or experience with software licensing/assets is a plus
Experience with market research tools and practices.
Working knowledge of financial and operational metrics/KPIs (key performance indicators) used by professional services firms.
Proficiency in MS Office (including advanced Excel and PowerPoint skills) and familiarity with various research tools (Factiva, Hoovers, Bloomberg, OneSource, D&B)
Experience in financial analysis with finance based KPIs.
Demonstrated strong analytical, project management, and organizational skills.
In-depth analytical abilities, including data analysis, issue identification, and resolution tracking.
Demonstrated ability to work independently and collaborate with a team in a dynamic, cross-functional work environment.
Detail-oriented with ability to plan, prioritize, and make decisions on multiple time-sensitive projects.
Exposure to Tableau/ Power BI tools will be a value add.
Clear and concise written and verbal communication skills.


Work Location -Hyderabad.

Timings - 2 PM – 11PM

Our purpose

Deloitte’s purpose is to make an impact that matters for our people, clients, and communities. At Deloitte, purpose is synonymous with how we work every day. It defines who we are. Our purpose comes through in our work with clients that enables impact and value in their organizations, as well as through our own investments, commitments, and actions across areas that help drive positive outcomes for our communities.

Our people and culture

Our inclusive culture empowers our people to be who they are, contribute their unique perspectives, and make a difference individually and collectively. It enables us to leverage different ways of thinking, ideas and perspectives, and bring more creativity and innovation to help solve our clients’ most complex challenges. This makes Deloitte one of the most rewarding places to work.

Professional development

At Deloitte, professionals have the opportunity to work with some of the best and discover what works best for them. Here, we prioritize professional growth, offering diverse learning and networking opportunities to help accelerate careers and enhance leadership skills. Our state-of-the-art DU: The Leadership Center in India, located in Hyderabad, represents a tangible symbol of our commitment to the holistic growth and development of our people. Explore DU: The Leadership Center in India .

Benefits To Help You Thrive

At Deloitte, we know that great people make a great organization. Our comprehensive rewards program helps us deliver a distinctly Deloitte experience that helps that empowers our professionals to thrive mentally, physically, and financially—and live their purpose. To support our professionals and their loved ones, we offer a broad range of benefits. Eligibility requirements may be based on role, tenure, type of employment and/ or other criteria. Learn more about what working at Deloitte can mean for you.

Recruiting tips

From developing a stand out resume to putting your best foot forward in the interview, we want you to feel prepared and confident as you explore opportunities at Deloitte. Check out recruiting tips from Deloitte recruiters.

title: Business Analyst & Project Manager
description:
This role is for one of the Weekday's clients

Salary range: Rs 500000 - Rs 2000000 (ie INR 5-20 LPA)

Min Experience: 5 years

Location: Hyderabad

JobType: full-time

You will lead the delivery of customer projects by utilizing AI-driven, AI-centered methodologies. Serving as the liaison between clients—mainly based in the UK—and our internal engineering teams, you will ensure top-quality results, clear communication, and comprehensive documentation throughout every phase. This position requires someone capable of fostering genuine trust with Western clients, managing complex project challenges, and motivating teams to achieve ambitious goals. You will blend insightful business analysis with practical project management, all while advocating for our AI-driven delivery approach.

Requirements

What You'll Be Responsible For


Oversee the end-to-end delivery of client service engagements, from initial scoping through to final sign-off
Serve as the main point of contact for Western clients, cultivating trust and long-term partnerships
Create clear, well-organized documentation, including diagrams, prototypes, and detailed specifications
Maintain project momentum by managing timelines, resolving blockers, and ensuring teams meet quality standards
Enable effective communication between clients and internal engineering, design, and AI teams
Keep up to date with trends in software engineering, cloud infrastructure, AI tools, and emerging technologies
Support pre-sales activities by assisting with proposal scoping, effort estimation, and solution presentations
Promote our AI-led delivery philosophy throughout all project processes


What We Are Seeking


More than 5 years of experience in business analysis, project management, or client-facing delivery roles
Demonstrated experience working with UK or Western clients—candidates who have studied or worked in the UK are particularly encouraged to apply
Outstanding verbal and written communication skills, with an ability to quickly establish trust and rapport
Strong skills in documentation and illustration, comfortable with wireframing, prototyping, and technical writing
Familiarity with databases, cloud infrastructure, programming environments, and modern technology stacks
Ability to engage socially, build friendships within teams and with clients, while driving accountability and delivering results
Comfortable managing ambiguity, conducting difficult conversations, and keeping projects on track
A genuine passion for AI, software engineering trends, and how technology is transforming delivery methods

title: Junior Data Analyst
description:
Role Overview

The Junior Data Consultant/Analyst is responsible for supporting end-to-end data analysis activities, from initial collection to final stakeholder reporting. This role focuses on transforming raw data into actionable business insights through advanced spreadsheet modeling and emerging data automation tools.




Key Responsibilities

Data Preparation & Cleaning: Identify, extract, and clean data from diverse sources including spreadsheets and internal databases to ensure accuracy and consistency.
Advanced Analysis: Utilize statistical techniques to detect trends, patterns, and correlations within large datasets to support organizational goals.
Consulting & Collaboration: Work with cross-functional teams to define business problems, track key performance indicators (KPIs), and recommend process improvements.
Reporting & Visualization: Develop structured reports, interactive dashboards, and visual presentations to communicate findings clearly to non-technical stakeholders.
System Maintenance: Assist in the upkeep and documentation of data collection systems and databases to maintain data integrity.




Technical Requirements

Advanced Excel (Must-Have): High proficiency in complex formulas (e.g., XLOOKUP, INDEX/MATCH), PivotTables, and data modeling is essential for daily analysis tasks.
Preferred Tools (Pluses):
SQL: Ability to write queries to extract and manage data from relational databases.
Python: Experience with libraries like Pandas and NumPy for data manipulation and automation.
Alteryx: Hands-on experience creating automated workflows for data wrangling and ETL processes.
Education: Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative field such as Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Computer Science, or Information Management.

Show more

title: HR Analyst (Freelancer)
description:
About Us:

At Deccan AI Experts, we operate at the intersection of human intelligence and artificial intelligence. Our team brings together talent from premier institutions and diverse industries to build scalable, high-quality data solutions that power next-generation AI systems. With a global presence and a strong execution hub in India, we are driven by curiosity, speed, and meaningful impact.




Role Overview:

We are seeking a detail-oriented and insight-driven HR Data Analyst who can transform people data into strategic insights. This role goes beyond reporting—you will play a key part in shaping workforce strategies by uncovering patterns, identifying opportunities, and enabling smarter HR decisions.




What You’ll Do:

Translate complex HR data into clear, actionable insights for business and HR leaders.
Build and maintain dashboards to monitor workforce trends, hiring effectiveness, and employee lifecycle metrics.
Analyze patterns related to attrition, engagement, performance, and productivity.
Partner with HR teams to optimize processes such as hiring, onboarding, and retention.
Support strategic initiatives like workforce planning and organizational design using data-backed recommendations.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and integrity across HR systems.




What We’re Looking For:

Experience in HR analytics, people analytics, or a similar data-focused HR role.
Strong command of tools such as Excel, Google Sheets, SQL, or BI tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau).
Ability to interpret data and communicate insights in a simple, business-friendly manner.
Problem-solving mindset with attention to detail.
Comfortable working in a fast-paced, remote, and project-based environment.




Why Join Us?

Work at the forefront of AI-driven operations and decision-making.
Gain exposure to diverse HR challenges across projects and industries.
Flexible, remote-friendly work structure.
Opportunity to directly influence organizational strategy through data

title: Senior Business Analyst
description:
About the Company

We are seeking a proactive Business Analyst with 3–5 years of experience who can define functional requirements, support project planning, and contribute to data-driven decision-making. The ideal candidate will have hands-on experience in writing functional specs, working with cross-functional teams, and basic proficiency in SQL and Power BI to support reporting and analysis needs.




Responsibilities:

Business & Functional Analysis: Elicit and document business and functional requirements through stakeholder discussions, workshops, and research.
Functional Specifications: Draft BRDs, FRDs, use cases, user stories with acceptance criteria, and system behaviour documentation.
SQL & Data Analysis: Use basic SQL to query data for validation, analysis, and stakeholder insights. Collaborate with developers and data teams for accurate data mapping.
Power BI Support: Assist in building or reviewing dashboards, validating data outputs, and supporting business stakeholders in leveraging BI reports.
Project Planning Assistance: Work with project managers to define scope, prioritize features, track milestones, and update timelines.
Stakeholder Collaboration: Act as a liaison between business users, tech teams, and QA to ensure shared understanding and smooth delivery.
Process Improvement: Identify inefficiencies and propose improvements through data-backed insights and process mapping.
Testing & UAT: Prepare test cases, support user acceptance testing, and ensure alignment with business expectations.

Qualifications:

Bachelor's degree in IT, Computer Science, or related field (MBA preferred).
3–5 years experience as a Business Analyst with exposure to full SDLC.
Solid understanding of writing functional specifications and process flows.
Basic knowledge of SQL – able to write simple queries, joins, and filters.
Experience using Power BI – able to interpret dashboards and assist in data storytelling.
Proficiency with tools like JIRA, Confluence, MS Visio, Excel, and PowerPoint.
Strong communication, analytical thinking, and documentation skills.




Required Skills:

Domain knowledge in BFSI, Healthcare, Logistics, Retail or Manufacturing (or any other industry).
Experience in Agile/Scrum delivery environments.
Familiarity with data pipelines, KPIs, and data validation.
Certifications like CBAP, CCBA.

title: Analyst
description:
Skill: Media Planning, DV360, Meta, Google ads

Experience: 2 to 4 years.

Location: Hyderabad, Bangalore, Chennai, Coimbatore.

Notice period: 30 to 90 days.




We are OGS—Omnicom's Integrated Solutions Engine that brings together a global talent community and connected capabilities in data, creative, media, commerce, and technology to build adaptive, future-ready solutions.

We are redefining the global capability center - not as an offshoring hub, but as a true inside edge. OGS is the place where the future is tested, built, and scaled for the world’s most ambitious agencies and clients.

With over 4300+ talented professionals in India, we are proud to be a diverse, inclusive, and collaborative community that thrives on innovation and excellence. Our teams work with some of the world’s most iconic brands, helping them move faster and achieve more—everywhere. We are growing rapidly and looking for passionate individuals to be part of this journey.

Let us build this, together!

About the role




This exciting role of a Media Planning – Analyst requires you to strategize and plan digital media campaigns for our client. You will collaborate with various teams, including account managers, creative teams, and data analysts, to develop effective and impactful digital media strategies that align with clients' goals and objectives.




Collaborate with account managers and internal teams to understand marketing objectives and develop digital media strategies
Conduct research on target audiences, industry trends, and competitive landscape to inform media planning decisions
Identify and evaluate digital media channels (e.g., display advertising, social media, search engine marketing, mobile advertising) based on their effectiveness in reaching target audiences and delivering desired outcomes
Collaborate with media buyers to negotiate favorable terms, including rates, placements, and added value opportunities with media vendors and publishers
Develop comprehensive media plans, including budget allocation, media mix recommendations, and campaign timelines.




Qualification:




A full-time graduate degree (Mandatory)
Proven experience (typically 2-3 years) in digital media planning, preferably in an agency or marketing environment
Strong understanding of digital marketing channels, including display advertising, social media, search engine marketing, and mobile advertising
Familiarity with advertising platforms such as
Bachelor's degree in marketing, advertising. communications, or a related field
Google Ads, Facebook Ads Manager, or programmatic platforms
Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities to identify insights.
Strong analytical skills and logical reasoning to manage daily deliverables
Excellent written and verbal communication skills

title: Data Analyst - Fresher
description:
About CogniView

Cogni View is a fast-growing staffing and technology services company, offering end-to-end recruitment and workforce solutions to businesses across industries. We pride ourselves on matching the right talent with the right opportunities through innovation and dedication.

About the Role




We are looking for a detail-oriented and analytical Data Analyst to collect, process, and interpret data to help drive business decisions. The ideal candidate should be comfortable working with data, identifying trends, and presenting insights clearly.



Key Responsibilities



Collect, clean, and analyze large datasets

Identify trends, patterns, and actionable insights

Create reports, dashboards, and visualizations

Work with stakeholders to understand data requirements

Perform data validation and ensure data accuracy

Use statistical techniques to interpret results

Automate data processes where possible

Present findings in a clear and concise manner


Required Skills & Qualifications



Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or related field

Strong knowledge of Excel (Advanced)

Proficiency in SQL for data querying

Basic knowledge of Python or R

Familiarity with data visualization tools (Power BI / Tableau)

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills

Good communication and presentation abilities


Preferred Skills (Bonus)



Experience with Python libraries (Pandas, NumPy, Matplotlib)

Knowledge of data cleaning and preprocessing techniques

Familiarity with business intelligence tools

Understanding of statistics and data modeling

Internship or project experience in data analysis


What We Offer



Competitive salary + performance incentives

Hands-on experience with real-time data projects

Learning and development opportunities

Growth in data analytics and business intelligence

Supportive and collaborative work environment


Who Can Apply?



Freshers with strong analytical skills

Candidates with 0–2 years of experience

Individuals passionate about data and insights

title: Financial Analyst I
description:
Description

GAR (Global Accts Rec) is looking for a proactive, customer and people centric Finance Team Leader to support the Collections process. The ideal candidate will be responsible for overseeing the collection efforts, ensuring that accounts are handled appropriately and communicate with clients as required to increase the percentage of on-time payments. This person is result-oriented, multitasker with strong communication skills. Foster team unity as you build relationships while working closely with your team and fellow managers to continually reach department goals

Key job responsibilities


Be an expert in the collection processes.
Support creation and implementation of strategies to increase the number of successful collections on past due invoices and increase the penetration of accounts
Handle communication with clients on complex scenarios to ensure good customer experience
Collect, track and present metrics, and drive process improvements
Troubleshoot and triage any technical issues during collections and report as needed for long term sustainability of the process
Contribute to improvements in collection projects by identifying any issues, proposing enhancements in tools and working on continuous improvement of collection protocol and process.
Communicate internally or externally to identify issues and propose solutions for long term sustainability of processes
Willing to create and implement standard operating procedures, quality guidelines and process workflows
Leading and developing a team of collection analysts, including hiring, training, and employee development
Managing group workload , establishing priorities and performing regular audits to ensure existing policies and procedures are followed
Managing annual performance targets, creating and monitoring appropriate performance metrics
Act as an escalation point in resolving complex outstanding issues with internal and external stakeholders


Basic Qualifications


Bachelor's degree or above in finance, accounting or related field
In depth knowledge of Order to Cash Process
6+ years of experience in Accounts Receivables with at least 1 year of people management and collections experience
Demonstrate ability to lead diverse talent within a team, work cross-functionally, and build consensus on difficult issues
Excellent negotiation, interpersonal, verbal and written communication skills
Ability to work under pressure and with strict deadlines
Proficient in Microsoft Excel and Word
Experience in driving process improvements and operational efficiency projects


Preferred Qualifications


Post-graduation or a Master’s Degree Accounting/Finance or a related field


Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. title: Invoice-to-Pay and Accounts Payable Analyst
description:
Job Title: Invoice-to-Pay and Accounts Payable Analyst

Location: Hyderabad

At Sanofi we chase the miracles of science to improve people’s lives. We are dedicated to making a positive impact on the lives of the patients and families we serve, and we accomplish our goals through world-class research and with the compassion and commitment of our employees.

As we continue to transform the practice of medicine, the next chapter of Sanofi’s Play to Win strategy will require a focus on delivering transformation and simplification of our core processes, optimizing resource allocation and deployment to fuel business growth and investment in science.

To facilitate this transformation, a new Business Operations Business Unit is being established, bringing together existing business service activities, and driving further expansion of centralized services at scale into a global unit, with a focus on driving simplification, efficiency, and productivity.

This new Business Unit will enable the delivery of best-in-class business support capabilities across the organization, incorporating and engaging disparate teams from across areas such as R&D, M&S, the Corporate Functions and GBUs into new global Service Delivery Towers with initial services spanning Commercial, Finance, Procurement and People & Culture.

Reporting to the Head of S2P Delivery, the Invoice-to-Pay and Accounts Payable Analyst plays a critical role in managing the accounts payable end-to-end process of from invoice receiving, invoice processing to invoice payment. This position involves also the execution of month-end-activities ensuring the accurate and timely payment to suppliers while adhering to financial policies and regulatory requirements.

Responsibilities


Invoice Receiving: Ensure in collaboration with service providers and Digital team proper receiving of invoices through inbound channels.
Invoice Processing: Efficiently process invoices, verifying accuracy, and ensuring compliance with relevant policies and collaborate with internal stakeholders and BPO to resolve discrepancies and obtain necessary approvals for complex cases.
Accounts Payable Management & Month End Closing: Manage in collaboration with the BPO the account payable process, including the timely and accurate recording of financial transactions and reconcile accounts payable records with supplier statements and resolve discrepancies for complex cases.
Payment Processing: Coordinate in collaboration with the BPO and internal teams the payment process, ensuring timely disbursement to suppliers and validate payment requests against approved invoices and payment terms .
Compliance and Documentation: Ensure adherence of all processes in scope to financial policies, regulatory requirements, internal controls, and S2P Core Model and maintain organized and accurate documentation related to invoice-to-pay processes.
Issue Resolution: Address and resolve issues related to invoices, payments, and accounts payable and communicate with suppliers and internal teams to resolve payment-related queries.


Qualifications

Required:


Previous experience in accounts payable, invoice processing, or related financial roles.
Familiarity with financial systems and tools from business perspective and technical fundamentals: ERP (SAP preferred), invoice workflow tools.
Strong attention to detail and accuracy.
Effective communication and interpersonal skills.
Transversal competencies and ability to work across processes, geographies, languages, cultures, business maturity levels.
Strong commitment to continuous process improvements
Ability to prioritize and manage multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment.
Fluent in English (spoken and written).


Preferred


Knowledge of ERP systems, such as SAP.
Understanding of financial and procurement best practices.
Degree in finance, accounting, or a relevant field.

title: Trainee Associate Financial Analyst
description:
Description & Requirements

Electronic Arts creates next-level entertainment experiences that inspire players and fans around the world. Here, everyone is part of the story. Part of a community that connects across the globe. A place where creativity thrives, new perspectives are invited, and ideas matter. A team where everyone makes play happen.

The CFO organization comprises a broad group of finance disciplines, with the common goal of partnering with business teams delivering strategies and providing our players with amazing experiences. Our finance teams assist the business in achieving profitable creativity by proving financial insight, reporting and guidance.

We use progressive tools to analyze and visualize data, telling the story of financial drivers, performance, risks and opportunities. We value attributes like collaboration and learning. We celebrate diversity and inclusion by creating a place where you can come to work and be yourself.

Trainee - Associate Financial Analyst

Job Type: Internship for 06 months – to be converted into a Full-Time Hire depending on performance

Note

The Internship will be for 06 months during which a stipend is paid and based on the performance

This opportunity is ONLY for fresher’s who are pursuing Final or have completed their full-time MBA/PGDM/CIMA/CMA – Finance Specialization with 0 to 1 year of Pre-qualification Experience. Someone with good communication skills and basic Excel Skills would be given preference.

Role Overview

Finance plays a pivotal role in monitoring the company’s financial performance and ensuring all systems are in place to track current activity and forecast forward

I. Basic Function

Supporting the FP&A team with reporting, analysis and forecasting

II. Responsibilities And Duties

Responsible for proactively supporting the needs of the business

Support monthly forecasting process through updating of information in Global Planning System

Own reporting solution development to establish standard processes and centralized reporting solutions.

III. Qualifications

Pursuing Final /Completed - MBA / PGDM / CIMA / CMA– Finance Specialization

IV. Qualities / Experience

0-1 years of Finance related pre-qualification experience

Effective communication

Working knowledge of MS Office – Excel & PowerPoint

Problem solving ability

Analytical ability

Understanding of Financial statements

Theoretical understanding of financial budgeting

Team Player

Quick learner

About Electronic Arts

We’re proud to have an extensive portfolio of games and experiences, locations around the world, and opportunities across EA. We value adaptability, resilience, creativity, and curiosity. From leadership that brings out your potential, to creating space for learning and experimenting, we empower you to do great work and pursue opportunities for growth.

We adopt a holistic approach to our benefits programs, emphasizing physical, emotional, financial, career, and community wellness to support a balanced life. Our packages are tailored to meet local needs and may include healthcare coverage, mental well-being support, retirement savings, paid time off, family leaves, complimentary games, and more. We nurture environments where our teams can always bring their best to what they do.

Electronic Arts is an equal opportunity employer. All employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, national origin, ancestry, sex, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, genetic information, religion, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, marital status, family status, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We will also consider employment qualified applicants with criminal records in accordance with applicable law. EA also makes workplace accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities as required by applicable law.

title: Business Analyst-Healthcare Payer
description:
Job Description

We are seeking an experienced Business Analyst with extensive expertise in the Healthcare Payer domain to join our team as an SME.




Responsibilities

Act as a trusted SME for Healthcare Payer processes, policies, regulations, and industry best practices
Elicit and analyze complex business requirements from cross-functional stakeholders, including payers, providers, and other entities
Define/Write the functional specifications, process flows, and use cases to guide solution design and delivery
Collaborate with IT, QA, and business teams to align on project scope, objectives, and deliverables
Perform gap analyses to pinpoint areas for process optimization and system enhancements
Participate in workshops and stakeholder meetings to gather insights, validate requirements, and drive alignment
Translate intricate business processes into clear, actionable technical documentation that can be used by developers
Provide expert guidance during solution implementation to ensure compliance and alignment with business goals.
Support stakeholders through training and change management initiatives




Requirements

Bachelor’s degree in Science, or a related field
Minimum of 5 years of experience within the Healthcare Payer domain
Deep understanding of payer operations including claims processing, enrolment, member management, provider networks, and benefit administration
Knowledge of the Business Process, Good Understanding of EDI
Strong understanding of healthcare data formats, standards, and can map them to CMS and state level reporting needs.
Knowledge of healthcare regulations and compliance standards (e.g., HIPAA, CMS, ACA)
Expertise with payer systems such as claims management, enrolment platforms, billing, and provider portals
Exceptional communication and stakeholder engagement skills, Written and Spoke communication Skills
Familiarity with Agile and Scrum methodologies
Proficiency with Business Analysis tools and techniques




Nice to have

Certified Business Analysis Professional (CBAP)
Healthcare-specific certifications such as CHC or CPCO

title: Business Analyst
description:
About the Role

We are hiring multiple Data Analysts to join our fast-growing analytics team. Experience a fast interview process with quick turnaround, working on high-impact problems that influence decisions for millions of users.

This role offers the opportunity to work with large datasets, drive business insights, and directly contribute to strategic decision-making across the organization.

About the Company

Swiggy is India’s largest on-demand convenience platform, operating across 500+ cities with a robust logistics network. From food delivery to groceries and hyperlocal services, Swiggy powers seamless digital experiences for millions of users across the country.

Key Responsibilities

Analyze large and complex datasets to solve business problems
Monitor, forecast, and track key business metrics
Design and run experiments to test business hypotheses
Perform root cause analysis and generate actionable insights
Collaborate with business, product, and data teams to improve data quality

Skills & Experience

Experience in analytics, data science, or related fields
Proficiency in SQL, Python, R, or Excel
Familiarity with BI tools such as Power BI or Tableau
Understanding of basic statistical concepts and measurement frameworks
Ability to work with large datasets in fast-paced environments

What We’re Looking For

Strong problem-solving and analytical thinking
Ability to work in unstructured, high-energy environments
Effective communication and storytelling with data
Strong collaboration and stakeholder management skills

Apply Even If…

You don’t meet every requirement listed above. We encourage you to apply if you’re passionate about solving business problems using data.

Additional Highlights

Work on real-world business problems with measurable impact
Exposure to cross-functional teams and decision-making processes
Be part of a fast-paced, high-growth environment

title: Business Analyst
description:
About the Role:

This individual will provide backend office support, data analysis & visualization and reporting assistance to the Business Head. The position involves data collection, report preparation, coordination with sales and field teams, and support in in driving business through analytics in different domains viz sales, competition, mapping, cost benefit analysis, productivity - derived from system.

Collect data from sales teams, branches, and internal departments for ongoing projects and reports.
Coordinate with team for timely completion of assignments given by Business Head, taking required reports on or before time, and update Business Head
Proactively engage with Business Head and team for preparing annual Business plan / sales forecast
To analyze future business plan (sales, marketing, competition, benchmark, business forecast, future challenges etc.) as part of Strategic Business reviews at Management level.
Deep analysis of success and failures in adherence to the targets & to monitor projects & their progress by tracking status of activities, resolving problems, generating progress reports, recommending actions in consultation with the Business Head
Analyze investment v/s benefit of various Market development / Marketing Activities undertaken by the company for bringing more focus on time bound results.
Consolidate data from multiple sources and prepare analytical reports to highlight gaps and progress.
Coordinate with regional and branch teams for status updates and follow-ups on ongoing initiatives.
Help plan and organize meetings, prepare presentations, and record Minutes of Meeting (MoM).
Maintain trackers, reports, and documentation in a structured manner.
Support preparation of management presentations using Excel, PowerPoint, and Power BI.

Working knowledge of MS Excel, PowerPoint, Power BI, Sales force, MIS and SAP.
Proficient in ppt preparation, analytical and reporting skills.
Good coordination and follow-up abilities.
Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet timelines.

Opportunity to work with a well-established brand
Strong communication and interpersonal skills

5–7 years of experience in reporting, coordination, backend support, or similar roles.

We are hiring multiple Analytics Engineers to join our fast-growing data team. Experience a fast interview process with quick turnaround, working on large-scale data systems that power decisions across millions of users.

This role offers the opportunity to work at the intersection of data engineering and analytics, building scalable data solutions that directly impact business outcomes.

title: Research Analyst
description:
Job Purpose

To recommend and advise investors for various investment ideas from medium to long term perspective by doing quantitative and qualitative analysis of listed companies. To contribute to the team activities in generating investment ideas, creating and maintaining large and complex databases, help in preparing various reports like strategy note, morning note, weekly market updates. These activities will help in driving sales and information dissemination to investors and sales force.

Job Context & Major Challenges

Aditya Birla Money Ltd. (ABML) is primarily a Broking Company, deals in Equity, Commodity & Currency. Workforce of ~800 spread across ~40 locations with customer base of in excess of 3 lacs. Commodity operations are managed through its 100% subsidiary Aditya Birla Commodities Broking limited. ABML is one of the leading securities trading companies in the country. ABML operates in a fragmented equity broking business and has a daily turnover of close to 3,000 Crores with a market share of 1%. ABM works in a highly regulated environment with a host of regulators like SEBI, RBI, AMFI, FMC, IRDA etc. regulating its day to day operations. In addition to the regulator ABML is also subject to close monitoring and inspection through various agencies like NSE / BSE / NCDX / MCX / NSDL / CDSL etc. ABML also operates in a highly competitive market mainly in fragmented industry with ABML competition from major National Broking houses like ICICI Securities, HDFC Securities, Angel Broking, MOFSL etc. and a range of local Broking outfits.

Majority of the broking houses have set up in-house research team that scans companies and stocks as well as analyse macro-economic scenario that impacts the stock market. The Research team at the Fundamental side values the securities based on the growth prospects and attractiveness of the underlying business. It involves analyzing the financials, competitive advantages of the business, quality of management and competitive environment it operates in among other factors. The theory of Research revolves around the fact that the market price of an asset may deviate from its fair value in short term due to extraneous factors such as market trends or investor sentiments, but will tend to move towards its intrinsic value over the long term.

The Key Responsibilities Of Sr. /Research Analyst Are


To build large databases by extracting and collating appropriate datasets from data vendors like Bloomberg, Reuters, Cline, etc.
To analyse large and complex databases of macro and micro information and create well-structured reports.
To disseminate research reports/output to investors/clients helping them take investment decisions.
To provide research support and inputs for consumption by the investment team in building portfolios and prepare reports/advisory for the clients.
To timely work on and generate investment ideas so as to capture maximum gains out of it.


The Primary Challenges Faced Are


To find investible ideas based on rigorous analysis of data/information sourced from quality database available in the market within a sector, given different risk return profiles and competitive advantages of various companies within a sector.
In a an environment, which is constantly moving and changing, the key challenge is to stay abreast with latest updates in a sector and the companies within it, so as to take nimble investment decisions.
The challenge is to clearly communicate the investment thesis for a company/sector in a manner which clearly highlights the investment case and arguments so that the investment team/investors can take an informed investment decision.
Another key challenge of the role is to take relative investment calls when absolute opportunities are more than what can be accommodated in the portfolio


Critical Skillsets are the individual must have a strong knack for financial analysis and equity research. The role involves minor level of client interaction and hence good interpersonal skills are desirable.

Key Result Areas

KRA (Accountabilities) (Max 1325 Characters)Supporting Actions (Max 1325 Characters)

KRA1 To be able to build, maintain and analyze large and complex databases of macro and micro information


To build large databases by extracting/collating appropriate datasets from data vendors like Bloomberg, Reuters, Cline, etc. and organize the data into sub-heads (viz. macros / micros / sectoral / valuations).
To understand the growth trends of the industry by analysing parameters affecting the dynamics of the industry.
To analyze data using top-down approach (international asset class level, country level and sector level) and also on a bottom up basis on assigned sectors to come up with investment ideas for the investment team and clients.


KRA2 To be able to conduct quantitative and qualitative analysis of the companies of specific sectors and participate in team research activities


To analyze companies/sectors using tools like ratio analysis, time series and cross sectional analysis, so as to make a judgment regarding investment potential of the various sectors and the companies within various sectors.
To read relevant materials from the industry body and participate in industry conferences to maintain the sharpness in investment analysis.
To meet the managements of a company and understand the key trends in the sector, the company and its future plan.
To project the earnings of the company in medium and long term.


KRA3 To be able to disseminate research based output to investors/clients helping them take investment decisions


To prepare an investment argument based on the findings of the analysis and help the investment team build a portfolio as per the risk/return framework of the investor.
To contribute to various products which require team efforts like strategic investment report, morning report, morning sales note, concalls, weekly report.
To increase the visibility of the research team by contributing articles to various media reports.
Show more

title: Planning Analyst
description:
We are Licious and we are a Bengaluru based meat and seafood company founded in 2015 by Abhay Hanjura and Vivek Gupta, our founders, foodies and friends. We pride ourselves on being India’s most successful D2C food-tech brand operating in 20 cities across the country delighting over 32 lac customers with our de-licious fresh meat and seafood!




Think you have what it takes to be the magic ingredient in the recipe that is Licious? Read on..




Have you got an awesome appetite for it?




Supply & Inventory Planning Support:

● Support conversion of demand forecasts into supply, sourcing, and inventory plans across processing, dispatch, and last-mile nodes.

● Track and improve inventory metrics including availability, wastage, and liquidation, and highlight risks proactively to relevant stakeholders.

● Assist in maintaining inventory norms and planning parameters for fresh and perishable products




Cross-Functional Stakeholder Management

● Act as the primary planning point of contact for operations teams across Processing, Dispatch, and Last Mile for day-to-day planning alignment

● Coordinate closely with Demand Planning, Sourcing, Procurement, and City Operations team

to ensure supply plans are aligned and clearly communicated

● Work with Product and Engineering teams to articulate planning requirements, validate data outputs, and support rollout of new tools or reports

● Facilitate regular planning reviews, sourcing discussions, and issue-resolution forums

with cross-functional stakeholders




Network & Execution Alignment

● Support inventory placement and replenishment decisions by coordinating inputs across

hubs, cities, and central teams.

● Monitor execution vs plan and work with stakeholders to resolve deviations related to capacity, supply constraints, or demand volatility

● Ensure planning decisions are documented, communicated, and understood across teams




Automation & Process Enablement

● Support automation and reporting initiatives by gathering business requirements, validating

logic, and assisting with UAT and adoption

● Work with Product and Engineering teams to ensure planning tools reflect operational realities and constraints.




Business & Initiative Support

● Provide planning and coordination support for new initiatives, pilots, and scale-up programs.

● Assist partner teams in evaluating feasibility, capacity impact, and execution risks.




We hope that you have..

● 1–3 years of experience in supply chain, planning, analytics, or operations roles

(fresh /perishable supply chains preferred)

● Strong stakeholder management and communication skills; ability to work across multiple teams and influence without authority

● High attention to detail with ability to track multiple workstreams simultaneously

● Comfort working with data, dashboards, and planning tools (Excel, SQL, BI tools preferred)

● Structured problem-solving mindset and ability to escalate issues with clarity and context.

title: Commercial Business Analyst
description:
Job Description



WHAT YOU'LL DO



As Commercial Business Analyst, you are responsible for driving the strategic insight work and analysis of our merchandising through advanced data, customer and consumer insights in regard to our product. You will independently be able to set the scope and initiate analysis deepening on the need of the merchandising organization, demonstrate leadership, analytical prowess, and a deep understanding of industry trends.​



​You have a deep interest in retail analytics, inventory optimization and merchandising strategy development. You are customer and sales-driven professional with a strategic mindset. You thrive working in a fast pace, in and agile environment. You are natural team player that strives to foster collaboration across channels, roles and functions.​



Key responsibilities​



Conduct in-depth analysis of sales trends, customer behaviors and market dynamics to identify growth opportunities and areas for improvement; using statistical models and forecasting techniques to drive data driven decision-making.​



You stay abreast of industry trends, competitor product strategies, and emerging technologies to challenge current plans, maintain competitive advantage and support executional excellence by supporting with market intelligence in how to position, activate and optimize our offers.​



Analyze (and align with Customer Activation) SEO insights to support the SEO with our assortment strategy in close collaboration with merchandising managers / ecommerce leads as well as drive insights to support assortment strategy for all our channels; retail, digital and marketplaces.​



You drive projects and build business cases for new product categories, sales channels and other business opportunities to support new sales and profit growth.​



Follow up on our full (omni, retail and ecom) commercial initiatives and build strong case for improvement in our discount strategy, leveraging web analytics, customer insights, sales and cost data; enabling best possible commercial decision.



Please click here for complete role description



​WHO YOU'LL WORK WITH



You will collaborate with the colleagues from Merchandising team.



WHO YOU ARE​



To be successful in the role as Commercial Business Analyst, you should have strong commercial focus and are insight and data driven, with proven ability to drive results and work towards goals. You should have a broad merchandising knowledge and commercial experience that understands the total picture from customer demands on our offers.​



​What you need to succeed:​



Bachelor's degree, preferably in Economics or Business degree/relevant qualifications.​



Strong in driving commercial product excellence, strategic and analytical, with deep experience in business case building as well as follow up and estimate performance.​



Understanding of product, stocks and lifecycle management.​



High level of salesmanship and analytical proficiency; is an advance excel user.​



Effective collaborator with strong stakeholder management and communication skills.



Knowledge & experience in the Indian fashion retail.



WHO WE ARE​​



H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.​ ​



We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future. ​ ​



WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING HERE ​​



At H&M Group, we are proud to be a vibrant and welcoming company. We offer our employees attractive benefits with extensive development opportunities around the globe. ​​



You will receive 25% off H&M Group brands; H&M, COS, Monki, Weekdays, Arket, & Other Stories
We are an inclusive company where you are encouraged to be yourself at work


Additional Information



This is a full-time position, reporting to Regional Head of Merchandising.



Apply by sending in your CV in English as soon as possible, but no later than April 24th, 2026. Due to data policies, we only accept applications through career page.



Show more

title: Data Analyst
description:
A data analyst is responsible for organizing data related to sales numbers, market research, logistics, linguistics, or other behaviours. They utilize technical expertise to ensure data is accurate and high-quality. Data is then analyzed, designed, and presented in a way that assists individuals, businesses, and organizations make better decisions.

Using automated tools to extract data from primary and secondary sources

Removing corrupted data and fixing coding errors and related problems

Developing and maintaining databases, data systems – reorganizing data in a readable format

Performing analysis to assess quality and meaning of data

Filter Data by reviewing reports and performance indicators to identify and correct code problems

Using statistical tools to identify, analyze, and interpret patterns and trends in complex data sets that could be helpful for the diagnosis and prediction

Assigning numerical value to essential business functions so that business performance can be assessed and compared over periods of time.

Analyzing local, national, and global trends that impact both the organization and the industry

Preparing reports for the management stating trends, patterns, and predictions using relevant data

Working with programmers, engineers, and management heads to identify process improvement opportunities, propose system modifications, and devise data governance strategies.

Preparing final analysis reports for the stakeholders to understand the data-analysis steps, enabling them to take important decisions based on various facts and trends.
Show more

title: Product Analyst
description:
About the Team:




You’ll work with a team that’s reimagining how India interacts with a bank - powered by some of the smartest minds and the most intuitive tech in the ecosystem.




If you love impact, autonomy, and velocity, this is your playground




About the Role:




As an Analyst at slice, you’ll turn data into decisions. You’ll work with teams across Growth, Credit, Product, and Leadership to uncover insights, solve real business problems, and drive strategy. If you’re excited about using data to fuel impact at scale, this role is for you.




What You’ll Be Do:




Own the Full Analytics Spectrum




Think from First Principles: Break problems down to their core, use data and reasoning to uncover insights that move the business and make life simpler for our customers.

Descriptive – Build clear, compelling visualizations and dashboards.
Diagnostic – Dig into the why behind trends and performance.
Prescriptive – Turn insights into recommendations that drive smart decisions.

Drive Impact at Scale

Take full ownership of complex, high-impact problems that shape key org KPIs and influence strategic direction.
Lead end-to-end analytics projects in close partnership with Product, Business, and Strategy teams — from framing the problem to shaping solutions that drive measurable outcomes.

Partner with Leaders Across Teams

Work side by side with functional leaders and business partners across Growth, Retention, Product, Strategy, Engineering, Marketing, Fraud, and Ops — asking the right questions, challenging assumptions, and helping define the real business problems worth solving.
Tell Stories That Drive Action: Turn complex data into simple, actionable stories — crafting solutions that power big decisions and deliver real impact at scale.




What You'll Need:




Hustle + Heart: You’ve got the drive to build data-driven solutions that touch millions of lives - and the curiosity to keep asking “what’s next?”
1-3 years of experience solving complex business problems, uncovering insights, and driving key decisions that move metrics at scale.
AI-Native Mindset: Comfortable using tools like Cursor, Claude, or CoPilots to boost your speed, scale, and smarts.
You speak data fluently: Python and Advanced SQL are your second languages, and you’re comfortable in PySpark, R, or Excel when the situation calls for it.
Problem-solver at your core: You love breaking big, messy problems into bite-sized pieces — and crafting smart, scalable solutions that stick.
Natural Storyteller: You don’t just present numbers — you craft narratives that align stakeholders and inspire action.
Always learning: Whether it’s a new tool, technology, or technique, you’re excited to expand your toolkit.
Visualization is your superpower: Whether it’s dashboards or decks, you know how to turn numbers into a story people instantly get.




Life at slice:




Life so good, you’d think we’re kidding:




Competitive salaries. Period.
An extensive medical insurance that looks out for our employees & their dependants. We’ll love you and take care of you, our promise.
Flexible working hours. Just don’t call us at 3AM, we like our sleep schedule.
Tailored vacation & leave policies so that you enjoy every important moment in your life.
A reward system that celebrates hard work and milestones throughout the year. Expect a gift coming your way anytime you kill it here.
Learning and upskilling opportunities. Seriously, not kidding.
Good food, games, and a cool office to make you feel like home. An environment so good, you’ll forget the term “colleagues can’t be your friends”.
We believe in equality. Period.




At slice, we are committed to building a diverse and talented workforce. We never discriminate on the basis of race, sex, religion, colour, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, medical condition, disability, or any other class or characteristic protected by the applicable law.




We consider all qualified job-seekers with criminal histories in a manner consistent with the applicable law. Additionally, we are committed to providing reasonable accommodations to qualified individuals with physical or mental disabilities in order to participate in the job application or interview process, perform essential job functions, and receive other benefits and privileges of employment.




Come join our crew!




About us




slice

A new bank for a new India




slice’s purpose is to make the world better at using money and time, with a major focus on building the best consumer experience for your money. We’ve all felt how slow, confusing, and complicated banking can be. So, we’re reimagining it. We’re building every product from scratch to be fast, transparent, and feel good, because we believe that the best products transcend demographics, like how great music touches most of us.




Our cornerstone products and services: slice savings account, slice UPI credit card, slice UPI, and slice business are designed to be simple, rewarding, and completely in your control. At slice, you’ll get to build things you’d use yourself and shape the future of banking in India. We tailor our working experience with the belief that the present moment is the only real thing in life. And we have harmony in the present the most when we feel happy and successful together.




We’re backed by some of the world’s leading investors, including Tiger Global, Insight Partners, Advent International, Blume Ventures, and Gunosy Capital.

Show more

title: Product Analyst
description:
Sprinklr is the definitive, AI-native platform for Unified Customer Experience Management (Unified-CXM), empowering brands to deliver extraordinary experiences at scale — across every customer touchpoint.

By combining human instinct with the speed and efficiency of AI, Sprinklr helps brands earn trust and loyalty through personalized, seamless, and efficient customer interactions. Sprinklr’s unified platform provides powerful solutions for every customer-facing team — spanning social media management, marketing, advertising, customer feedback, and omnichannel contact center management — enabling enterprises to unify data, break down silos, and act on real-time insights.

Today, 1,900+ enterprises and 60% of the Fortune 100 rely on Sprinklr to help them deliver consistent, trusted customer experiences worldwide.

Job Description


Product Analysis & Insights
Analyze product usage, workflow behavior, support tickets, and customer feedback to identify trends and improvement opportunities
Build structured insights on feature adoption, configuration patterns, and failure points
Support data-driven prioritization and decision-making
Workflow & Product Understanding
Develop a deep understanding of workflow-driven and configuration-heavy product systems
Map user journeys, identify friction points, and recommend improvements to simplify workflow creation and execution
Understand system dependencies, edge cases, and fallback mechanisms
Product Management & Requirement Definition
Work closely with Product Managers to define problems and shape solutions
Create clear and detailed PRDs, functional specifications, and product documentation
Contribute to defining success metrics and evaluating post-release impact
Cross-functional Execution
Collaborate with Engineering, Design, QA, and customer-facing teams to drive feature execution
Ensure clarity of requirements and alignment during development and release cycles
Support issue resolution and iteration post-launch
Customer & Stakeholder Collaboration
Partner with Support, Managed Services, and Implementation teams to understand customer pain points
Convert recurring issues and escalations into actionable product improvements
Communicate product behavior and decisions effectively to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Documentation & Enablement
Identify and address documentation gaps
Contribute to internal and external documentation, best practices, and product guides
Improve product understanding and reduce avoidable support dependencies
What Makes You Qualified
1–2 years of experience in Product Management, Business Analysis, or similar roles within a SaaS company
Strong understanding of product management fundamentals (problem definition, prioritization, requirements, and execution)
Good understanding of workflow-based or configuration-driven systems
Strong analytical and structured problem-solving skills
Ability to operate effectively in ambiguous and fast-paced environments
Excellent communication and cross-functional collaboration skills
High ownership mindset with strong attention to detail


We focus on our mission: Sprinklr was founded in 2009 to solve a big problem: growing enterprise complexity that separated brands from their customers. Our vision was clear: to unify fragmented teams, tools and data — helping large organizations build deeper, more meaningful connections with the people they serve. Today, Sprinklr has a unified, AI-native platform for four product suites: Sprinklr Service, Sprinklr Social, Sprinklr Marketing, and Sprinklr Insights. Sprinklr is here to do three things:


Lead a new category of enterprise software that we call Unified-CXM.
Empower companies to deliver next generation, unified engagement journeys that reimagine the customer experience.
Create a culture of customer obsession, with trust, teamwork, and accountability.


We believe in our product: Customers who value exceptional customer experiences have what they need on our single unified platform, built with an operating system approach on a single codebase. That means that everything — and everyone — can work together to service, respond, sell, and market to customers on the channels they prefer. While Unified Customer Experience Management (Unified-CXM) as a category is just getting started, we are well on our way to creating a no-compromise, unified approach to better customer experiences for the world’s leading enterprise brands.

We invest in our people: We offer a comprehensive suite of benefits designed to help each member of our team thrive. Sprinklr believes that you should be able to get the type of care you need for your personal well-being when you need it. We offer you and your family voluntary healthcare coverage in countries where applicable. We believe it is important to take time off – it is essential for your mental and physical wellbeing. We provide Sprinklrites with paid time off to recharge and spend time with loved ones. We want to grow our talent with purpose. Our open Mentoring Program is designed to create meaningful connections that support growth and amplify our focus.

To learn more about employee benefits by region, click here.

To learn more about all-things-Sprinklr, visit our candidate resource hub here.

EEO - Our philosophy: Our goal is to ensure every employee feels like they belong and are operating in a collaborative environment. We fervently believe every employee matters and should be respected and heard. We believe we are stronger when we belong because collectively, we’re more innovative, creative, and successful. 

Sprinklr is proud to be an equal-opportunity workplace and complies with all applicable federal, state, and local fair employment practices laws. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, religion, creed, national origin or ancestry, ethnicity, sex (including gender, pregnancy, sexual orientation, and gender identity), age, physical or mental disability, citizenship, past, current, or prospective service in the uniformed services, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected under applicable law.

Reasonable accommodations are available upon request during the interview process. To request an accommodation, please work directly with your recruitment coordinator or recruiter.

Warning about Recruiting Scams: Please be vigilant for recruiting scams impersonating Sprinklr. Sprinklr will never ask you for money, to pay for equipment, or for unnecessary personal information during the interview process. Sprinklr will also never pay in Bitcoin or send email communications from our executives. Please review the Federal Trade Commission's advice to avoid these types of scams.

If you are contacted by someone whom you suspect may not be appropriately representing Sprinklr, please do not engage and block their email or phone number immediately.
Show more

title: Senior Product Analyst/Product Analyst
description:
Job Title: Senior Product Analyst – Notifications, Email & Retention

Department: Product / Growth

Experience Required: Minimum 3 Years

Location: Gurgaon




About the Role

We are looking for a Senior Product Analyst to drive data-led decision making across our Notifications, Email, and Retention initiatives. This role will partner closely with Product Managers, Growth, Marketing, and Engineering teams to analyze user behavior, optimize engagement channels, and improve customer retention across the ecommerce lifecycle.

The ideal candidate combines strong analytical skills, deep SQL expertise, business understanding, and an AI-first mindset to unlock insights and scale personalization across customer communication channels.




Key Responsibilities

Own analytics for customer engagement channels including Push Notifications, Email, and lifecycle communications.
Analyze user behavior across the ecommerce funnel to identify opportunities to improve retention, repeat purchases, and engagement.
Build and maintain SQL-based data models, dashboards, and reports to track key product and retention metrics.
Design and analyze A/B tests and experiments for notifications, email campaigns, and personalization strategies.
Partner with Product Managers to define success metrics and evaluate feature performance.
Develop insights on user cohorts, segmentation, and lifecycle behavior to improve CRM and engagement strategies.
Work with marketing and growth teams to optimize campaign targeting, frequency, and messaging effectiveness.
Leverage AI tools and AI-first thinking to automate analysis, generate insights faster, and improve personalization strategies.
Present insights and recommendations to stakeholders and influence product roadmap decisions.




Required Skills

SQL (Advanced): Strong ability to query large datasets and build analytical models.
Excel / Google Sheets: Advanced data manipulation, analysis, and reporting.
AI-First Thinking: Experience using AI tools for data exploration, analysis automation, and productivity.
Strong understanding of product analytics, experimentation, and user lifecycle metrics.
Experience with cohort analysis, funnel analysis, and retention metrics.
Strong communication skills with the ability to translate data insights into business recommendations.




Preferred Skills

Experience with product analytics tools (Amplitude, Mixpanel, GA4, etc.).
Experience working with CRM platforms or marketing automation tools.
Familiarity with Python or data visualization tools (Tableau, Power BI, Looker, etc.).
Experience in ecommerce or marketplace businesses.




Key Metrics You Will Impact

Customer Retention Rate
Repeat Purchase Rate
Notification / Email CTR & Conversion
User Engagement & Lifecycle Activation




Why Join Us

Opportunity to work on high-impact growth and retention problems in a fast-scaling ecommerce platform.
Collaborate with product, growth, and marketing teams to drive measurable business outcomes.
Build AI-enabled analytics capabilities that power smarter product decisions.




Reporting To: Product / Growth Leadership

Employment Type: Full-Time

title: Business Analyst
description:
Organization Unit Purpose




Group Transaction Banking, which includes Core Trade, Supply Chain & Receivable financing, Escrow and Cash Management and Merchant ACquiring. Transaction banking has been identified as one of the growth areas within ENBD Group. Transaction banking business coverage is across all international branches. The Group focus is to develop capabilities in Transaction banking across the group and generate fee income. Transaction banking values customer relationships and works closely with our clients to help them route their Transaction banking business with ENBD. Transaction banking business focus is delivery of innovative and world class products through our channels such as web/ mobile/ H2H/ Swift amongst others.




Job Purpose




Merchant Acquiring Business Analyst is responsible for analyzing business requirements, defining functional specifications, and supporting the implementation of acquiring solutions across POS and E-commerce channels. The role ensures business objectives are clearly translated into system capabilities while maintaining operational efficiency, compliance, and customer satisfaction.







Strategic:

• Gather, analyze, and document business and functional requirements for merchant acquiring processes.

• Conduct process analysis and gap assessments to identify improvements in acquiring workflows and system functionalities.

• Define end-to-end process flows covering merchant onboarding, transaction processing, settlement, reconciliation, dispute handling, and reporting.

• Collaborate with Product, Business, Operations, Risk, IT, Compliance, and Finance teams to ensure alignment on system requirements and business priorities.

• Support the design of new product features and enhancements within the acquiring ecosystem.

• Assist in developing operational procedures, documentation, and training materials for internal stakeholders.

• Ensure all processes and solutions comply with regulatory, risk, and card scheme requirements.

• Provide analytical insights and recommendations to enhance product performance and operational efficiency.







Ongoing:




• Be the decision making member of senior representatives from business and IT in categorizing the migration change management tasks into Agile and Platform streams.

• In collaboration with the Product Owners, manage scope and deliver the program scope as agreed/committed in the program, balancing pragmatism with core product functionality, without compromising the original intent of the product

• Be responsible for creating User Training plans, manuals and training methodologies

• Own the business user acceptance testing and build a core business test team around the migration extraction, validations, and reconciliation tasks.

• Own the end to end change management process for which includes, BRD, Product Development, Testing and rollout

• Be responsible for resolving day to day issues raised by various stakeholders, by putting up a robust production support and management structure including both business support teams and IT support teams.

Risk Management/Operative effectiveness & Controls

To deliver product and services that fully meet internal control measures and which comply fully with legal and compliance and regulatory requirements.
To continually reassess the operational risks associated with the role and inherent in the business, Taking account of changing economic or market conditions, legal and regulatory requirements, operating procedures and practices, management restructurings and the impact of new technology.
In conjunction with the Compliance department, to implement the Group compliance policy. This will include all relevant financial services laws, rules and codes with which the business has to comply.
